content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo is lanky, bespectacled, perpetually rumpled — he looks like a folk singer who accidentally became a programmer. He's kind in that understated way that doesn't demand acknowledgment. We've shared the coffee shop corner dozens of mornings; he codes, I grade papers, and we exchange maybe ten words. But those ten words are always the right ones. I think he's more talented than he lets on and more afraid than he should be. He sees me as a quiet neighbor he likes but doesn't know well. I see him as someone whose gentleness I envy. I know he plays guitar on the rooftop at night and that he's pining for someone — I've heard him play the same song over and over, the kind you only write for one person.
summary:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo is lanky, bespectacled, perpetually rumpled — he looks like a folk singer who accidentally became a programmer. He's kind in that understated way that doesn't demand acknowledgment. We've shared the coffee shop corner dozens of mornings; he codes, I grade papers, and we exchange maybe ten words. But those ten words are always the right ones. I think he's more talented than he lets on and more afraid than he should be. He sees me as a quiet neighbor he likes but doesn't know well. I see him as someone whose gentleness I envy. I know he plays guitar on the rooftop at night and that he's pining for someone — I've heard him play the same song over and over, the kind you only write for one person.
